Part one

The problem

Where Longhorn stands today, and what every quiet month is costing.
BridgeCom Narratives · 2026
The Problem
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Problem · 01
The truth, in one sentence

You do good work.
The market does not know you exist.

Longhorn has product, licence, expertise, and a real broker model that legally serves the client, not the institution. None of that is visible online. A buyer searching for an insurance broker in Lusaka today will not find Longhorn on Google, on LinkedIn, on Facebook, on Instagram, or on WhatsApp. They will find your competitors.
Longhorn × BridgeCom

Five platforms.
One number on each.

LinkedIn

WhatsApp

This is not a small problem to fix later. Every channel above is where a decision-maker in Lusaka decides who to call, who to trust, and who to pay. Longhorn is absent from all of them.

The Problem · 03
Where the decision happens

Zambia decides
in the open tabs.

WhatsApp is not a social platform here. It is the place a quote becomes a policy. Facebook is where trust accumulates. Google is where intent is captured. LinkedIn is where corporate cover is sold. None of these are optional. All five must work together.

05 / 22
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Problem · 04
The category trap

Say "insurance" in Zambia
and you inherit every broken promise the category has ever made.

Complexity. Distrust. Disappointment. The category's reputation is its largest liability. Any insurer or broker that markets itself in the language of "comprehensive cover," "competitive rates" and "peace of mind" walks straight into that baggage and is judged against it.
Longhorn is not an insurer. Longhorn is a broker, structurally required by law to work for the client, not the institution. That single fact is the most powerful brand asset in your portfolio, and right now nothing online is making it visible.
Longhorn × BridgeCom

Part Two
02
Part two

The reframe

The story Longhorn must tell, and how it must be told, before any tactic begins.
BridgeCom Narratives · 2026
The Reframe
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Solution · 01
The single most important strategic move

Stop selling insurance.
Start selling certainty.

What the category says
"Comprehensive insurance solutions, tailored to your unique needs."
What Longhorn says instead
"When life goes wrong, it does not have to stay that way."
The product is the same. The promise is the same. The way it is perceived, and the type of person willing to act on it, is entirely different. Certainty is human. Certainty is the thing people actually want.
Longhorn × BridgeCom
08 / 22
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Solution · 02
The brand promise

On your side.
Always.

Every insurer works for their shareholders. Every bank works for its balance sheet. A broker is legally required to work for the person sitting across the table. That is not a marketing claim. It is a structural fact that no competitor can honestly match. The entire brand is built on making it visible, credible, and human.
Longhorn × BridgeCom
09 / 22
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Solution · 03
Three audiences. One brand truth.

Different fears. Different messages.
Same Longhorn.

Segment A · Corporate

HR Managers, CFOs, Founders

Companies of 20 – 500 staff in Lusaka. They have cover. They are not satisfied. They value responsiveness and a single point of contact who understands their business.
Their core fearThe cover does not pay. Their career is on the line.
Segment B · SME

Owners & self-employed professionals

1 – 20 staff. Freelancers, contractors, traders. Chronically underinsured because they assume cover is unaffordable or too complicated to be worth it.
Their core fearOne fire, one theft, one accident ends everything they built.
Segment C · Households

Urban professionals 25 – 45

Already pay premiums, usually bundled through a bank or employer, and have no idea what their policy actually covers. Digitally active. Open to being educated.
Their core fearThey are paying for something they do not understand.
Longhorn × BridgeCom

BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Solution · 04
The reframe, applied to every product

Stop describing the product.
Describe what it does in someone's life.

ProductWhat the category calls itWhat Longhorn says instead
Motor "Third party, comprehensive cover." Your car is how you earn.Cover that means one accident does not stop your income.
Business / Fire "Commercial property, fire and perils." You spent years building this.One night should not end it.
Group Life "Group life assurance, funeral benefits." The benefit that tells your team:we will not abandon your family.
Medical "Medical aid, health insurance." The choice of which hospital your family goes to ,not which one you can afford.
Home "Household contents, building insurance." Everything inside that door took years to build.Covered for less than K 500 a month.

The plan

BridgeCom Narratives · 2026
The Plan
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Plan · 01
Architecture

Five channels.
One funnel. One CRM. One conversation.

01
Authority & awareness, organic contentLeadership posts, claims proof stories, education carousels, bursary programme. Owned. Free. Builds the foundation that paid spend stands on.
Owned · LinkedIn, FB, IG
02
Targeted reach, paid amplificationLinkedIn Lead Gen for corporate. Facebook & Instagram for retail and SME. Google Search for intent capture. Boost only what is already working organically.
Paid · K 11,760 – 24,360 / mo
03
Capture, enquiry into CRMEvery form, ad, page, and search routes into one CRM. Lead source, campaign, keyword tagged automatically. No spreadsheet. No leakage.
HubSpot / Zoho · Zapier
04
Convert, WhatsApp auto-reply & nurture60-second automated reply. Broker contact within SLA. 22-day nurture sequence for slow leads. Every touch warm, named, human.
SLA · 60s auto / 1h human
05
Compound, referrals, renewals, organicQuote becomes policy. Policy becomes testimonial. Testimonial becomes the next campaign. Acquisition cost falls every quarter the system runs.
The flywheel

BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Plan · 02
Each channel does one job

No channel does everything.
Each does one thing for the funnel.

B2B · Decision-makers
LinkedIn
Gets in front of the person who signs the contract.
Targets HR Managers, CFOs and Founders by job title, industry and company size, in Lusaka. The highest CPL in the plan. The highest LTV.
4–8leads / mo by M6
Retail · SME · Trust
Facebook & Instagram
Builds the audience that warms before it buys.
Reaches working Lusaka aged 25 – 50. Long-form trust on Facebook. Visual credibility and Reels on Instagram. Click-to-WhatsApp ads remove the landing page entirely.
10–20leads / mo by M6
Intent · Capture
Google
Catches the person who is already looking.
Three campaigns: broker intent, product intent, problem-aware. Each on its own dedicated landing page that matches the query exactly. Lowest volume, highest conversion.
3–6enquiries / mo by M6
Conversion · The close
WhatsApp
Where the quote becomes a policy.
Every other channel exists to move someone into a WhatsApp conversation with a Longhorn broker. 98% open rate. 22-day nurture sequence. Weekly broadcast list.
300+subscribers by M6
Longhorn × BridgeCom
14 / 22
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Plan · 03
Behavioural design, applied at every interaction

Every touchpoint reduces resistance.
Every touchpoint increases action.

01
Loss aversion
People are twice as motivated by avoiding a loss as gaining a benefit. Every headline leads with what is at stake, specific, local, vivid, before what is gained.
02
Friction reduction
Four-field forms maximum. WhatsApp CTA on every page. LinkedIn Lead Gen pre-filled from profile. Every step is reviewed: is anything here making the next move harder?
03
Social proof
Every claims proof story has a name, a business type, a location, and a specific outcome. "A Lusaka logistics company" beats "a business in Zambia" every time.
04
Trust heuristics
Named brokers, PIA licensing visible, consistent identity, SLA-fast response. People cannot evaluate insurance quality before buying, they evaluate trust proxies.
05
Optimism bias correction
SME owners assume the bad thing happens to other people. We make the worst case specific and real, then make the next step ridiculously easy to take.
Longhorn × BridgeCom

Part Four
04
Part four

The journey

Eighteen months. Four stages. Honest about what each one produces and what it does not.
BridgeCom Narratives · 2026
The Journey
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Journey · 01
Eighteen months, end to end

From invisible to compounding.
One stage at a time.

Stage 01
Months 1 – 3
Foundation
Build the infrastructure. Website, CRM, WhatsApp automation, social profiles, brand voice, analytics. No paid spend yet, there is nowhere for it to land.
OutputA credible brand online and a system to capture every enquiry.
Stage 02
Months 4 – 7
Activation
First paid campaigns. First Cover Check roadshow. First corporate risk briefings. SME workshop series. First two referral partners. Revenue begins here.
Output10 – 25 enquiries / month. First policies signed from digital.
Stage 03
Months 8 – 12
Optimisation
Data drives every decision. Pause what fails, scale what works. CPL falls month over month. Pipeline reviewed weekly by leadership. SEO begins to land.
Output30 – 50 enquiries / month. First full year-one KPI review.
Stage 04
Months 13 – 18
Compounding
The system reinforces itself. Organic reach grows without proportional spend. Referrals multiply. Brand awareness reduces friction at every stage of the funnel.
Output50+ enquiries / month. CPL down 30%+ vs Stage 2.

BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
Part Five
05
Part five

The promise

What BridgeCom will commit to in writing, and what we hold ourselves to internally.
BridgeCom Narratives · 2026
The Promise
BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Promise · 01
KPI philosophy

We promise what the industry average delivers.
We plan to significantly exceed it.

Every KPI presented to Longhorn is set at or below global industry average benchmarks for financial services in emerging markets. This is deliberate. The targets we commit to in the contract are the floor we will not miss. The targets we work to internally are materially higher.

An agency that over-promises and under-delivers loses the client at month three. An agency that under-promises and over-delivers earns trust, renewals, and referrals. The latter is the only commercially sustainable model.
The floor, in the contract
What we put in writing and report against monthly. Calibrated to emerging-market averages. Credible. Conservative. Comparable.
The ceiling, inside the team
What BridgeCom's team works to hit. Roughly 2× the floor on most metrics. Tracked weekly, never shared in client reporting as a target.
If we miss the floor
After two consecutive misses, a written recovery plan presented before the third month begins. No hiding. No excuses. A plan.
Longhorn × BridgeCom

The Investment · 01
Year-one investment, all categories

Every kwacha,
accounted for.

CategoryAnnual low (ZMW)Annual high (ZMW)Paid to
One-off setup (months 1 – 2)K 50,500K 78,200BridgeCom Narratives Ltd, website design, brand voice, CRM build, WhatsApp automation, analytics stack, initial content library, profile optimisation.
Monthly retainer × 11 months (M2 – M12)K 390,500K 594,000BridgeCom Narratives Ltd, strategy, content, paid campaign management, community, PR, SEO, reporting, CSR support.
Technology & tools (annual)K 15,624K 31,260Direct to platforms, CRM, WhatsApp API, scheduling, design, SEO, automation. Longhorn owns every account.
Advertising spend × 10 months (M3 – M12)K 117,600K 243,600Direct to LinkedIn, Meta and Google, never marked up by BridgeCom. Optimised monthly against CPL.
Video & production (as commissioned)K 45,600K 90,000Local production partners, broker videos, client testimonials, ceremony documentation, photography. Approved per shoot.
Year-one total rangeK 619,824K 1,037,060Full programme · All channels · All categories
Retainer minimum: six months. Monthly invoicing, 15-day terms. Ad spend invoiced separately and paid directly to platforms for full transparency. Longhorn owns every account, every contact, every asset, at all times.

BridgeCom NarrativesFor Longhorn Insurance
The Investment · 02
The return, in plain numbers

The programme is designed to pay for itself
out of its own monthly output.

All-in monthly cost (avg)
K 51k – 86k
Year-one total divided by 12. Agency fees, tools, ad spend, production, everything.
Monthly premium, M12 (committed)
K 80k+
From digital-sourced clients at the contracted floor. Stretch target: K 200k+.
What that means
Break-even
By Month 12, the digital programme covers its own monthly cost. Everything beyond that, referrals, renewals, organic compound, is the long-term return.
This is the conservative case. Stretch performance, which the team works to hit internally, delivers K 200,000+ in monthly premium revenue by M12 against the same cost base. Compounding into year two, the cost-per-enquiry falls 30%+ as organic and referral channels mature.

Close
Closing

Longhorn owns everything.
BridgeCom holds nothing back.

All accounts & admin rights
Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, WhatsApp Business, Google Ads, Analytics, CRM. Longhorn is the primary owner; BridgeCom has agency access only.
All assets, data & contacts
Website design files, every piece of content produced, the CRM contact database, the WhatsApp opt-in list. If the engagement ends, Longhorn keeps all of it.
Next step
A two-hour working session with Longhorn leadership, to walk every channel, every KPI and every line of the budget before signature.
